summary: Imported the Rust `dadosod` decompilation/disassembly helper under `tools/dadosod` as a workspace member. The PR mostly vendors the tool, its GPL-3.0-or-later license, README, Cargo metadata/locks, and source files for DOL disassembly, symbol CSV loading, PowerPC label/register tracking, section naming, macro/linker-script generation, and data/text/BSS assembly emission. The PR body says the author expected to reference and modify it for tooling; there were no review comments in the available slice.

key_files.reason: Adds `tools/dadosod` to the root Cargo workspace members.
Updates the root lockfile with `dadosod` and dependencies such as `argh`, `csv`, `serde`, `ppc750cl`, and `dol`.
Defines the imported `dadosod` package, GPL-3.0-or-later license, Wii/GameCube decompilation/disassembly metadata, and pinned git dependency on `ppc750cl`.
Documents intended features: DOL disassembly, section-name guessing, per-section files, BSS splitting, partial data type detection, and linker script generation.
Defines the `argh` CLI entry point and `dadosod dol` subcommand dispatch.
Implements DOL processing: read DOL, calculate section names, load optional CSV map, analyze sections, write `asm`, `include/macros.s`, translation-unit assembly files, and `ldscript.lcf`.
Adds the PowerPC GPR/label tracker used to identify branches, split address loads, SDA references, generated labels, and formatted assembly output.
Defines CSV-deserializable `Symbol` records with aliases for map fields like `VirtualAddress`, `Name`, `TranslationUnit`, and `Size`.
Provides small helpers for section flags and alignment.
Carries the GPL v3 license text for the imported subtree.

what_changed: Added `tools/dadosod` as a new Rust crate in the repository tooling tree.
Registered `tools/dadosod` in the root Cargo workspace.
Added both a subtree-local `tools/dadosod/Cargo.lock` and updates to the root `Cargo.lock` for workspace resolution.
Added a CLI with `dadosod dol <dol_file_path>` and options for output path, map file, translation-unit writing, and unreliable function detection.
Added DOL disassembly flow that reads a Wii/GameCube `.dol`, guesses section names, analyzes text/data sections, and writes assembly, macro, and linker script outputs.

Added handling for section names and CodeWarrior/MetroWerks-like ordering such as `.init`, `extab_`, `extabindex_`, `.text`, `.ctors`, `.dtors`, `.file`, `.rodata`, `.data`, `.bss`, `.sdata`, `.sbss`, `.sdata2`, and `.sbss2`.
Added symbol CSV ingestion with serde aliases to tolerate multiple capitalization/name variants used by map exports.
Added a GPR tracker that identifies branch destinations, split `lis` plus low-load address materialization, SDA references through r2/r13, and labels for known addresses.
Added assembly formatting fallbacks for illegal or assembler-problematic instructions by emitting `.4byte` with comments.
Added `.gitignore` entries for `target`, IDE metadata, `.DS_Store`, generated `.dol/.rel/.rso`, and `.exe` artifacts.
smart_moves: Kept the imported tool under `tools/dadosod` and made it a Cargo workspace member, so future modifications can build through the project’s existing Rust tooling path.
Pinned the `ppc750cl` dependency by git revision in `tools/dadosod/Cargo.toml`, reducing ambiguity for PowerPC disassembly behavior.
Preserved upstream package metadata and GPL-3.0-or-later licensing rather than importing source without license context.
Used `argh` subcommands with room for future `Rel` and `Rso` commands, matching the README’s planned REL/RSO expansion without implementing unsupported paths yet.

Made CSV symbol loading tolerant of field aliases such as `globalAddress`/`VirtualAddress`, `translationUnit`/`TranslationUnit`, and `localAddress`/`SectionAddress`, which is useful when consuming map data from different tools.
The tracker labels split-address forms with suffix-aware label kinds (`@ha`, `@h`, `@l`, `@sda21`) instead of leaving raw immediates everywhere.
The DOL section-name inference encodes known CodeWarrior/MetroWerks section ordering and BSS/SBSS/SBSS2 reconstruction heuristics, making generated assembly closer to decomp project conventions.
decomp_lessons: For decomp tooling imports, add the tool as an explicit workspace member and update lockfiles in the same PR so builds are reproducible.
When generating starter assembly from a DOL, split output into reusable project artifacts: per-section `.s` files, `include/macros.s`, and a linker script.
Symbol-map readers should accept multiple CSV header spellings/capitalizations because map-export formats vary across pipelines.
Generated labels should follow predictable address-based names so later manual naming can replace them without ambiguity.
DOL BSS handling may need to reconstruct logical `.bss`, `.sbss`, and `.sbss2` from merged or size-derived section ranges rather than trusting a single raw DOL section as final.
PowerPC decomp disassemblers should track `lis` plus `addi`/`ori`/load-store pairs and SDA base registers to recover relocatable labels from raw immediates.
When an instruction cannot be safely expressed in assembler syntax, preserving the exact word with `.4byte` is safer than emitting assembly that may not reassemble.
naming_conventions: `dadosod dol` is the imported DOL disassembly subcommand name.
Auto-created entry symbol defaults to `__start` at the DOL entry point when no map file is provided.
SDA base macros are written as `_SDA_BASE_` for r13 and `_SDA2_BASE_` for r2 when detected.
Section-name inference uses common decomp/CodeWarrior names: `.init`, `.text`, `.ctors`, `.dtors`, `.rodata`, `.data`, `.bss`, `.sdata`, `.sbss`, `.sdata2`, `.sbss2`.
Generated fallback section names use numbered forms like `.text0` and `.data0` before heuristics assign conventional names.
Imported extab sections are named `extab_` and `extabindex_` because comments state the leading dot form can conflict with linker-generated sections.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Track branch destinations inside known DOL sections and emit symbolic branch labels instead of raw targets.
Optionally detect functions from calls/branches via the `detect_functions` flag, though the CLI docs mark this as unreliable.
Track `lis` register values and later low-half operations to compute full addresses and annotate operands with `@ha`, `@h`, or `@l`.
Detect r2 and r13 initialization from `ori` patterns and use them to label small-data references with `@sda21`.
Reject unaligned text addresses when deciding whether an address belongs to a text section.
Emit `.4byte 0x00000000` for likely alignment words and `.4byte ... /* <illegal> */` for illegal instructions.
Special-case `lmw` with rD 0 and suspicious `bc` encodings as raw `.4byte` comments because the assembler may not handle the mnemonic or the bytes may be data.
Write data with size-sensitive directives such as `.byte`, `.2byte`, `.4byte`, strings, and labels based on discovered label boundaries.
Generate linker-script section entries with flags from section type: text as `"ax"`, data as `"wa"`, and BSS as nobits.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice, so no reviewer guidance can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs modifying `tools/dadosod` after subtree import.
Search for uses of `dadosod dol` in project documentation or build scripts.
Look for follow-up changes to `GPRTracker`, `combine_split_load_value`, or SDA label handling.
Check whether later PRs removed the subtree-local `tools/dadosod/Cargo.lock` or changed workspace lockfile policy.
Search for PRs adding REL/RSO support or library splitting to `dadosod`.
